chunk_summary: Fortified rice is a key micronutrient intervention in India, supplied through government schemes like PDS, PM POSHAN, and ICDS. It delivers iron, folic acid, and vitamin B12 but requires careful clinical counseling for special populations and quality concerns.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of fortified rice in India's national nutrition programs.
Learn the clinical counseling guidelines for children receiving fortified rice.
Identify special considerations for children with hemoglobinopathies or iron overload states.
Recognize the importance of addressing quality concerns and public trust in fortified rice distribution.
key_facts: Fortified rice is blended with fortified rice kernels (FRK) at a 1:100 ratio.
Universal scheme coverage for fortified rice was achieved by March 2024.
Free supply of fortified rice under government schemes is approved until December 2028.
Fortified rice delivers iron, folic acid, and vitamin B12 but does not treat moderate or severe anemia quickly.
Clinical decisions for therapeutic iron should be based on Hb levels and individual status.
reference_quote: Universal scheme coverage was achieved by March 2024 and the free supply of fortified rice under government welfare schemes was approved for continuation from July 2024 to December 2028. (Reference: https://www.pmindia.gov.in/en/news_updates/cabinetapprovescontinuation-ofsupply-...)
document_key_topics: Fortified rice distribution through government schemes
Micronutrient delivery via fortified rice kernels
Quality and trust issues in fortified rice supply
